attempts.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)
    
        The image of the scene has been provided.
        Please first analyze the image and then generate the PDDL problem.
        
    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: Put a hot glass inside a cabinet.
    

SCENE CAPTION:
The scene depicts a modern kitchen environment with several key objects relevant to the robotic planning task. The primary focus is on a stainless steel sink, white cabinets, a microwave, and various objects placed within or near these receptacles.

1. **Objects and Their Types**:
   - **Receptacles**: There are three main receptacles in the scene:
     - A **sink** (stainless steel, embedded in a granite countertop with a geometric pattern), which can hold objects for cleaning.
     - Two **cabinets** (white, with silver handles, positioned below the countertop), which can store objects.
     - A **microwave** (stainless steel, mounted above the countertop), which can heat objects.
   - **Objects** (non-receptacles):
     - A **glass** (transparent, cylindrical, currently resting in the sink).
     - A **bottle** (transparent, with a narrow neck, also in the sink).
     - An **egg** (brown, oval-shaped, placed in the sink).
     - A **knife** (metallic, with a black handle, not visible in the provided images but implied by the domain as a tool for slicing).
   - **Agent**: The robot (not visually depicted) is the agent that will perform actions.

2. **Quantities of Each Object Type**:
   - Receptacles: 3 (1 sink, 2 cabinets, 1 microwave).
   - Objects: At least 3 distinct objects (1 glass, 1 bottle, 1 egg) are visible in the sink. The knife is not visible but is part of the domain.

3. **Spatial Relationships**:
   - The **glass**, **bottle**, and **egg** are all located inside the **sink**.
   - The **microwave** is positioned above the countertop, to the left of the sink.
   - The **cabinets** are located below the countertop, directly under the sink and extending to the right.
   - The **agent** (robot) is not currently at any object’s location, as per the initial state.

4. **Current State of Each Object**:
   - The **glass** is in the sink and is not yet cleaned, heated, or cooled.
   - The **bottle** and **egg** are also in the sink but are not relevant to the current instruction.
   - The **microwave** is not currently turned on (isOn is false).
   - The **cabinets** are closed (opened is false) and are not currently holding any objects.
   - The **sink** is not currently being used for cleaning (isClean is false for the glass).

5. **Colors, Positions, and Other Visual Properties**:
   - The **sink** is stainless steel with a reflective surface, embedded in a granite countertop with a light gray and white geometric pattern.
   - The **cabinets** are white with silver handles, positioned below the countertop.
   - The **microwave** is stainless steel with a digital control panel, mounted above the countertop.
   - The **glass** is transparent and cylindrical, resting in the sink.
   - The **bottle** is transparent with a narrow neck, also in the sink.
   - The **egg** is brown and oval-shaped, placed in the sink.
   - The **floor** is made of light-colored wood with a natural grain pattern.

The robot’s task is to put a hot glass inside a cabinet. This requires the robot to first go to the sink, pick up the glass, heat it in the microwave, and then place it inside one of the cabinets. The current state of the scene shows that none of these actions have been performed yet.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
```pddl
(define (problem put_hot_glass_in_cabinet)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        sink - sink
        microwave - microwave
        cabinet_left cabinet_right - receptacle
        glass bottle egg - object
        knife - knife
    )
    (:init
        (openable sink)
        (openable microwave)
        (openable cabinet_left)
        (openable cabinet_right)
        (not (opened sink))
        (not (opened microwave))
        (not (opened cabinet_left))
        (not (opened cabinet_right))
        (inReceptacle glass sink)
        (inReceptacle bottle sink)
        (inReceptacle egg sink)
        (not (holdsAny robot))
        (not (isClean glass))
        (not (isHot glass))
        (not (isCool glass))
        (not (isOn microwave))
        (not (isToggled microwave))
        (not (isSliced glass))
        (not (atLocation robot sink))
        (not (atLocation robot microwave))
        (not (atLocation robot cabinet_left))
        (not (atLocation robot cabinet_right))
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle glass cabinet_left)
        (isHot glass)
    ))
)
```
attempts.problem:
(define (problem put_hot_glass_in_cabinet)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        sink - sink
        microwave - microwave
        cabinet_left cabinet_right - receptacle
        glass bottle egg - object
        knife - knife
    )
    (:init
        (openable sink)
        (openable microwave)
        (openable cabinet_left)
        (openable cabinet_right)
        (not (opened sink))
        (not (opened microwave))
        (not (opened cabinet_left))
        (not (opened cabinet_right))
        (inReceptacle glass sink)
        (inReceptacle bottle sink)
        (inReceptacle egg sink)
        (not (holdsAny robot))
        (not (isClean glass))
        (not (isHot glass))
        (not (isCool glass))
        (not (isOn microwave))
        (not (isToggled microwave))
        (not (isSliced glass))
        (not (atLocation robot sink))
        (not (atLocation robot microwave))
        (not (atLocation robot cabinet_left))
        (not (atLocation robot cabinet_right))
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle glass cabinet_left)
        (isHot glass)
    ))
)
attempts.plan:
(gotolocation robot glass)
(pickupobject robot glass)
(gotolocation robot microwave)
(heatobject robot microwave glass)
(gotolocation robot cabinet_left)
(putobjectinreceptacle robot glass cabinet_left)
